excluded_sample_audit.resolution: Both disputed exclusions were re-adjudicated against the records' own abstracts and reinstated as included (C1315: a DRL agent selects budget-constrained bridge maintenance actions, Layer 3; C0129: the reinforcement-learning engine generates construction-sequencing recommendations, Layer 1). Both are abstract-only records whose rubric dimensions are largely not assessable.

dispositions: already in corpus via the primary search (C0728)

Automation in Construction 2026 article (10.1016/j.autcon.2026.106796), initially screened from its title; the abstract, subsequently obtained via institutional access (2026-07-17), confirms the exclusion: the learned components are a CTGAN data augmenter and nested DNNs performing supervised M&R treatment-type classification scored by decision accuracy against expert labels, a one-shot classification rather than a sequencing, assignment, or timing decision (contrast the included T0057, whose imitation-learned output is the intervention timing itself).
prenet_recall_audit.note: The one known keyword-pre-net miss during the review (T0056) was recovered by iteration-3 snowballing.

critical_records_dual_coding.summary: Blind second coding of the corpus's remaining top-baseline-tier and deployed studies not already covered by the gain-set dual coding. Agreement: layer 4/4, deployment level 3/4 (C0946 pilot-vs-field wording judgment; the study's own phrase 'field tests' retained), B 4/4 within the not-assessable convention (C1277 and C0065 are abstract-only and -1 on both sides of the comparison where scorable), E 3/4 (C0262 excerpt did not show the statistical tests the full text reports).

full_pass2_exclusion_audit.protocol: Every full-text-stage exclusion (n=83) was independently re-screened blind at title/abstract level by a second coder under the archived pass-1 rulebook (retrieval/screening/RULES.md); the second coder saw no recorded decisions or reasons.

dispositions.not_assessable_from_record_text: 19 records, mostly title-only dossiers and verifiability/integrity exclusions; each recorded exclusion rests on dossier, full-text, or citation-integrity evidence documented in the coded record
dispositions.duplicate_reports: 8 include-verdicts are duplicate reports of studies the corpus counts once under the canonical id (C0503->C0092, C0504->C0091, C0708 and C0912->S0228, C0966->C0230, S0196->C0797, C1145->C0509, C1671->C1265); the blind screener correctly recognized the underlying studies as eligible
dispositions.integrity_screen: 2 rest on citation-integrity evidence external to the record text (C0865: unverifiable authorship, predatory venue; C1655: character-identical abstract across disjoint author teams)
dispositions.readjudicated_upheld: 12 disputes re-adjudicated against the full-text dossiers and upheld: T0046, C0453, C0653 (learned component does continuous motion/mission control, not a discrete scheduling decision); C1542, C0724, C0935, C1257 (learned component predicts or estimates rather than making the scheduling decision); S0198, C0551 (unevaluated framework or position papers); S0226, S0227 (per-component intervention timing without schedule, assignment, or resource structure, the documented T0057 boundary); S0233 (spatial facility layout, not scheduling)

full_pass2_exclusion_audit.note: The earlier 15-record random audit (2026-07-17) had already reinstated the 2 disputed exclusions it surfaced (C1315, C0129); this full-coverage audit runs on the corrected corpus and overturned no exclusion. The two reports-not-retrieved reclassifications of 2026-07-20 postdate this audit and raise the exclusion ledger from 83 to 85.

pass1_dual_screen.protocol: A second coder independently re-screened a seeded random sample (Python random.Random(20260720).sample, n=200) of the 1,655 pass-1 title/abstract records under the archived rulebook, blind to all recorded decisions.
pass1_dual_screen.disposition: No adjudication changed final corpus membership. Pipeline-resolved: C0960, C0997, S0261, C1954, C0169 were later excluded at the full-text stage for the very concerns the second coder raised, and C1753 was integrity-excluded; C1321 was promoted and is included, as the second coder read it. C0827 is a Figshare data artifact of C0575, which the corpus includes. C0713 (included) was disputed as cross-sector; inclusion upheld on full-text evidence, and the cross-sector pooling is now stated in its coded record. C1316 (title-only) had its abstract retrieved during this audit and was recoded at abstract level, reassigning it L1->L2. S0289 and C1259 remain excluded under the domain rule (no built-environment framing in the record text); S0289 is flagged for an optional author full-text check. C1779 stays excluded on the recorded full-text rationale.

title_only_upgrade.action: Abstract retrieved verbatim from the RePEc mirror of the Springer chapter abstract; record upgraded to abstract-level coding; layer reassigned L1 -> L2 (work packages explicitly span production, transportation, and on-site assembly of industrialized construction); an independent blind coder reproduced the recode (layer, problem class, B=1 under the vagueness rule, E=0, D=0, simulation).
title_only_upgrade.remaining_title_only: C0465 and C0748 were subsequently (2026-07-20) reclassified as PRISMA reports-not-retrieved exclusions; see prisma_ledger.json not_retrievable_note.

top_tier_deployed_dual_coding.protocol: Blind independent second coding of the five top-tier or deployed studies not already dual-coded in the gain set, reliability sample, or critical-records set (C0408, C0665, C1660, S0158, S0242), on layer, problem class, strongest comparator, B/E/D, and deployment level. With this pass every study reaching the top tier of any rubric (B, E, or D = 3) or deployed at pilot/field level has been coded by two people.

disagreements.adjudication: primary upheld: full text (Section 5) reports held-out instances, size generalization, a statistical test, and an ablation (evaluation_protocol coded accordingly); the second coder worked from a 6000-character excerpt that truncated Section 5.

primary upheld; the difference is one tier and C0665's evaluation is already flagged in the main text as fragile (placeholder figures, missing hyperparameters). Not a deployed study, so no effect on the empty cell.
primary upheld: source_note cites paired t-tests p<0.01 plus held-out and distribution-shift evidence; truncated excerpt for the second coder.
primary upheld: held-out (100 arrivals x300), due-date-tightness distribution shift, and multiple seeds are in Section 5, beyond the excerpt.
primary upheld: instances are calibrated to precast production (D=1), a distinction not visible in the excerpt.

primary upheld: the strict B=3 bar requires a full suite (tuned rules AND an exact/metaheuristic AND, where one exists, a prior learned method); S0158 benchmarks against an exact MILP (SDDiP) but lacks the tuned-rule and prior-learned components, so B=2. The second coder read the exact-solver comparator alone as B=3.
primary upheld: out-of-sample, diverse topologies (distribution shift), and scenario-averaged with standard deviation (seeds) are reported; truncated excerpt.

top_tier_deployed_dual_coding.outcome: No coded value changed. Layer and deployment agreed for all five, so the central empty-cell finding (no pilot/field-deployed study faced a strong baseline) rests on no contested code; all five re-coded studies are simulation-only. The rubric disagreements were mostly on evaluation rigor and trace to the second coder scoring from a shorter excerpt than the primary full text, which is documented per record. The single baseline-tier disagreement that went the other way (S0158, second coder more generous) is a rubric-interpretation difference resolved by the stated full-suite definition of B=3.
